title: Control-M Forecast vs Actual End Time Variance
app: Control-M Forecast SLA attributes mapped into Splunk
spl:
index=cicd sourcetype="controlm:job"
| where isnotnull(forecast_end) AND isnotnull(end_time)
| eval variance_min=round((end_time-forecast_end)/60,1)
| where abs(variance_min) > 30
| stats avg(variance_min) as avg_var, p95(variance_min) as p95_var by job_name
| sort -p95_var
description: Systematic variance between forecast and actual drives SLA gaming or bad baselines.
value: Improves forecast tuning for financial and supply-chain batches.
implementation: Backfill baselines monthly; exclude one-off manual reruns via `run_as` filter.
knownFalsePositives: Busy or odd job states during code moves, new folder cutovers, one-off re-runs, or test folders still mixed into production; compare with the scheduler before you change how jobs are defined.
grandmaExplanation: We compare planned end times to when work really finished, so forecasts get better and we stop promising files we will miss.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure: Control-M Forecast SLA attributes mapped into Splunk.
• Data: `controlm:job` with `forecast_end` and actual `end_time` epoch fields.
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Backfill baselines monthly; exclude one-off manual reruns via `run_as` filter.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="controlm:job"
| where isnotnull(forecast_end) AND isnotnull(end_time)
| eval variance_min=round((end_time-forecast_end)/60,1)
| where abs(variance_min) > 30
| stats avg(variance_min) as avg_var, p95(variance_min) as p95_var by job_name
| sort -p95_var
```

Understanding this search
Systematic variance between forecast and actual drives SLA gaming or bad baselines.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the Control-M job plan and run history in the job scheduler. Reconcile job and folder names, start and end times, and queue or pool use for a few highlighted jobs.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Box plot (variance), Table (top drifting jobs).
